enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a casual vlog update from Boulder on October 10, 2017, describing a low-key day of working late, printing pictures, and returning to an old bike research project he had nearly abandoned months earlier. He also mentions watching Netflix, specifically a show or movie about a siege with impressive battle scenes.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: excited to revisit it because it was nearly done and his faster computer makes it easier
enjoys using it to watch just the best parts of movies

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder. Vlog for January 10th, 2017. Yesterday, it was a pretty normal day. I worked until like 6 or something until it was about to get light and then went to sleep and got up at like 2 and then worked.
And I was printing pictures and posting them on my walls, but also mainly working on my bike racer level stuff. Super exciting. It's getting more and more interesting the more I work on it.
And it's been a long time. I started working on it when I first moved to Boulder because I moved here and I looked out of the window and I saw the flat iron.
So I was like, oh my God, I want to be an athlete. So I was like, okay, let's build that. So I built it. And then for like forever, for months, I was working on that.
And then all of a sudden I cracked. I decided I didn't want to, I wanted to just work on my computer and do all sorts of other stuff.
And so I abandoned it. But I remember when I abandoned it, it was like almost done. It was out of all the projects I've ever done on my website.
That's the one that I abandoned the closest to being done. So it's super exciting to come going back in there and try to fix stuff.
I don't know how long it'll be, but it's worth directly at least a week. Like I said before to do that.
I was, it's kind of hard to explain what I was fixing, but when I built it, it was with my old computer, which is a whole lot slower than this.
So to fix it, it's a lot faster and it's a lot easier. And so it's exciting to bring together.
And then let's see, I was listening to a little bit of a history of Rome. I mean, the fall of Rome podcast, but I watched, I was watching TV.
I love having Netflix on this TV here. I watched this movie, the show called fall of the, or sack of siege of Vienna.
It has some great battle scenes. I want to watch those again. It's cool to go in and watch movies with Netflix because there's so many of them.
Just go and watch the best parts of all the interesting movies, you know, but, uh, yeah, it's very cool.
Okay. Let's see here. Like, yeah, I think I've talked long enough. I don't really feel like talking today.
I've been, it's like nine 38. Oh my God already. I never got to sleep last night.
So I'm starting to get a little bit, uh, tired.
